title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) at home with our authentic Indian recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for any occasion!
seo.h1: Best Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Butter Chicken
Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
Frequently Asked Questions About Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A delicious bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani), a beloved Indian dish perfect for a comforting meal.

description: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish perfect for a comforting meal. This authentic Murgh Makhani recipe features tender chicken simmered in a velvety tomato-based sauce, infused with aromatic spices and finished with a touch of butter and cream. Prepare to delight your senses with this iconic Indian classic, guaranteed to be a family favorite.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 1 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion, remaining 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Saute until onions are soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, soaked cashews (drained), sugar, and remaining 1/2 tsp garam masala. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (if easily found). Carefully transfer the sauce to a blender or use an immersion blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky sauce, pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the blended gravy to the pan over low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir in the remaining 2 tbsp butter, kasoori methi (crushed between your palms), and heavy cream. Simmer gently for another 5 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors and the sauce to warm through. Do not boil after adding cream.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Taste and adjust salt if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice for a truly authentic Indian meal.

tips: For best results, marinate the chicken overnight. The longer the marination, the more tender and flavorful your chicken will be.
Do not skip the Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves); it provides an essential, unique aroma and slightly bitter taste that defines authentic Butter Chicken.
Always blend the gravy until completely smooth. Passing it through a fine-mesh sieve can elevate the texture to restaurant quality.
article:
Discover the culinary magic of one of India's most beloved dishes – Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian chicken recipe has charmed palates worldwide with its rich, creamy tomato gravy, succulent chicken, and delicate balance of sweet and tangy flavors. Forget takeout; mastering this classic at home is a rewarding experience, transforming your kitchen into an aromatic haven of Indian spices. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every step, ensuring you create an authentic and irresistible Butter Chicken that will impress family and friends.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, translates literally to "chicken with butter," a name that perfectly encapsulates its indulgent essence. Originating in the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s, this dish was born from a need to utilize leftover tandoori chicken. Chefs tossed the grilled chicken pieces in a rich, buttery, and tomato-based gravy, inadvertently creating a global phenomenon. What sets Butter Chicken apart is its signature velvety sauce, a harmonious blend of pureed tomatoes, cashews, cream, and a medley of aromatic but mild spices. Unlike its spicier cousin, Chicken Tikka Masala, Murgh Makhani typically offers a milder heat profile, allowing the sweetness of tomatoes and the richness of dairy to shine through. It's comfort food at its finest, a symphony of textures and flavors designed to soothe and satisfy.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a few key components. Fresh, high-quality ingredients make all the difference in achieving that authentic restaurant-quality taste.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch cubes
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt (full-fat recommended)
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, less heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Murgh Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 large onion, roughly chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1.5 cups crushed tomatoes (from a can of whole peeled San Marzano tomatoes or fresh ripe tomatoes)
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in hot water for 15 minutes, then drained)
*   1/2 cup water
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (or more, to taste)
*   1 tablespoon sugar (or to taste, balances tomato acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Murgh Makhani

Follow these steps carefully to create a truly spectacular Butter Chicken:

Step 1: Marinate the Chicken
In a large bowl, combine the cubed chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Mix well, ensuring all chicken pieces are coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for maximum flavor.

Step 2: Cook the Chicken
Heat 1 tablespoon of butter or oil in a large skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes, flipping occasionally, until the chicken is lightly charred and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

Step 3: Prepare the Sauce Base
In the same pan (add a little more butter if needed), add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, and water. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and the cashews to soften further.

Step 4: Blend the Sauce
Remove the sauce base from the heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra velvety texture, you can strain the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan, pressing down on any solids.

Step 5: Finish the Curry
Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium heat. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter. Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric powder, and garam masala. Simmer the sauce for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the spices to cook and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the sauce. Stir in the kasuri methi (crushed between your palms to release its aroma), sugar, and heavy cream. Gently simmer for another 5 minutes, ensuring the chicken is thoroughly heated through and the flavors meld. Do not boil vigorously after adding cream, as it may curdle. Taste and adjust salt and sugar as needed.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

*   **Marination is Key:** The longer you marinate the chicken, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination yields the best results.
*   **Char the Chicken:** Don't skip the step of cooking the chicken separately and getting some char on it. This adds a crucial smoky depth that enhances the overall flavor of your Butter Chicken.
*   **Smooth Sauce Matters:** Blending the sauce thoroughly until absolutely smooth is vital for that signature velvety texture. Straining it afterwards takes it to another level.
*   **Good Quality Tomatoes:** Use good quality canned crushed or whole peeled tomatoes (like San Marzano) for a richer, less acidic sauce. If using fresh, ensure they are very ripe.
*   **Don't Overcook the Cream:** Add the cream towards the end and simmer gently. Boiling vigorously after adding cream can cause it to split.
*   **Kasuri Methi is Essential:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) impart a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic flavor that is characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani. Don't omit it! Toasting it lightly in a dry pan before crushing can intensify its flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Butter Chicken

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments. Here are some classic serving suggestions:

*   **Indian Breads:** Warm naan bread (garlic naan is a popular choice), roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up every last bit of that luscious gravy.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Garnish:** A generous sprinkle of fresh, chopped cilantro adds a burst of freshness and color. A swirl of extra cream or a small knob of butter can also be added just before serving for an extra touch of indulgence.
*   **Sides:** A simple cucumber raita (yogurt dip) or a fresh green salad can offer a refreshing counterpoint to the rich curry.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is undeniably delicious, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), firm tofu, or even mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Marinate paneer or tofu similarly and pan-fry before adding to the sauce.
*   **Dairy-Free Version:** Substitute regular yogurt with a dairy-free alternative (like coconut yogurt) in the marinade. Use full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of heavy cream, and vegan butter instead of dairy butter. The flavor profile will shift slightly but still be delicious.
*   **Spicier Kick:** For those who prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili along with the ginger-garlic paste.
*   **Smokier Flavor:** For an authentic tandoor-like smoky flavor, you can try the "dhungar" method: place a small, hot charcoal in a small metal bowl in the center of your finished curry, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for 5-10 minutes.
*   **Healthier Twist:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or use light cream. Marinating chicken in Greek yogurt can also add tenderness while being lower in fat.

## Frequently Asked Questions About Butter Chicken

**Q: What's the difference between Butter Chicken and Chicken Tikka Masala?**
A: While both are popular creamy tomato-based curries, Butter Chicken is generally milder, sweeter, and richer, emphasizing butter, cream, and pureed tomatoes. Chicken Tikka Masala often features a more robust spice blend, a slightly thicker, less creamy sauce, and typically uses chicken tikka (marinated and charred chicken pieces) which imparts a distinct smoky flavor.

**Q: Can I make Butter Chicken in advance?**
A: Absolutely! Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld. Store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. It also freezes well for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the fridge and reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or cream if it's too thick.

**Q: Is Butter Chicken naturally spicy?**
A: Traditionally, Butter Chicken is known for its mild and creamy profile, not its heat. The red chili powder primarily adds color and a gentle warmth rather than intense spice. However, you can easily adjust the spice level by adding more chili powder or fresh green chilies if you prefer a spicier curry.

**Q: What is Kasuri Methi and why is it important?**
A: Kasuri Methi refers to dried fenugreek leaves. It's a crucial ingredient in many North Indian dishes, including Butter Chicken, providing a unique, slightly bitter, earthy, and aromatic flavor that is distinctive and hard to replicate with other herbs. Crushing it between your palms before adding helps release its essential oils and maximizes its flavor contribution.

With this detailed guide, you're now equipped to recreate the magic of classic Murgh Makhani in your own kitchen. This Indian chicken recipe is more than just a meal; it's an experience, a testament to the rich culinary heritage of India. Enjoy the journey of making and savoring this incredibly flavorful and comforting Butter Chicken!
